arguments.command:
# Check specific known directories for git repos
for d in /Users/landonking/.openclaw /Users/landonking/.openclaw/workspace /Users/landonking/.agents; do
  if [ -d "$d/.git" ]; then
    echo "GIT: $d"
    cd "$d" && git remote -v 2>/dev/null | head -2
    echo "---"
  fi
done

# Also check common dev locations
for dir in /Users/landonking/dev /Users/landonking/projects /Users/landonking/repos /Users/landonking/code; do
  if [ -d "$dir" ]; then
    ls -d "$dir"/*/.git 2>/dev/null | sed 's/\/.git$//'
  fi
done

content.text:
Good — I have two repos with GitHub remotes:
1. **`~/.openclaw`** → `origin` = `github.com/landonking-gif/openclaw.git`
2. **`~/.openclaw/workspace`** → `github` = `github.com/landonking-gif/openclaw-workspace.git`

And `.agents` has no git repo yet. Let me initialize one for it, then push all three.

arguments.command:
# Check if the agents repo exists on GitHub; create if not, then add remote and push
cd /Users/landonking/.agents

# Check if remote already exists
git remote -v 2>/dev/null | head -2

# Add GitHub remote (assuming same org pattern)
git remote add origin https://github.com/landonking-gif/openclaw-agents.git 2>/dev/null || echo "remote may already exist"

# Create the repo on GitHub via gh CLI if available
which gh 2>/dev/null && gh repo create landonking-gif/openclaw-agents --private --description "OpenClaw agent skills and config" 2>&1 || echo "gh CLI not available or repo exists"
